name: Inline Raman Spectroscopy and Indirect Hard Modeling for Concentration Monitoring of Dissociated Acid Species
description: Raman spectra and composition data for titration experiments of various acids in aqueous solution. Includes acetic, citric, formic, itaconic, levulinic, oxalic, and succinic acids. Data for concentration monitoring and indirect hard modeling.
citeAs: @article{Echtermeyer_2020, title={Inline Raman Spectroscopy and Indirect Hard Modeling for Concentration Monitoring of Dissociated Acid Species}, volume={75}, ISSN={1943-3530}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1177/0003702820973275}, DOI={10.1177/0003702820973275}, number={5}, journal={Applied Spectroscopy}, publisher={SAGE Publications}, author={Echtermeyer, Alexander and Marks, Caroline and Mitsos, Alexander and Viell, Jorn}, year={2020}, month=dec, pages={506--519}}
citation: Echtermeyer, Alexander Walter Wilhelm; Marks, Caroline; Mitsos, Alexander; Viell, Jörn. Inline Raman Spectroscopy and Indirect Hard Modeling for Concentration Monitoring of Dissociated Acid Species. Applied Spectroscopy, 2021, 75(5):506–519. DOI: 10.1177/0003702820973275.

distribution.name: Inline Raman Spectroscopy and Indirect Hard Modeling for Concentration Monitoring of Dissociated Acid Species
distribution.description: Source data hosted at the original repository.

field.name: spectrum_id
field.description: Sequential integer index identifying each spectrum.

field.name: raman_shift_cm_inv
field.description: Raman shift wavenumber axis in cm⁻¹. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,).

field.name: spectrum_intensity
field.description: Raman scattering intensity in arbitrary units. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,) per spectrum.

field.name: target
field.description: Target value(s): concentration, physical property, or other continuous measurement.

rai:dataCollection: Inline Raman spectra acquired during NaOH (1 mol L⁻¹) titration of formic acid (Merck, 98–100%) in deionized water (0.8 μS cm⁻¹) at 25 °C using a Kaiser RXN2 Raman analyzer (400 mW, 785 nm) with NIR fiber-optic immersion probe, spectral range 160–3285 cm⁻¹, 4 cm⁻¹ resolution, 60 s acquisition time. Ambient light excluded by non-transparent black PVC liner. pH monitored continuously with SenTix 940 electrode and Multil 3420 pH meter (WTW).
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Standard Normal Variate (SNV) normalisation, linear baseline correction, and offset subtraction applied. Evaluation range 1025–1850 cm⁻¹, excluding atmospheric O₂ signal (1545–1565 cm⁻¹). Preprocessing in MATLAB R2017a (spectral subtraction) and PEAXACT ver. 4.5 (S-PACT).
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Mole fraction targets for two acid species (FA, FA⁻) derived from pH-based dissociation equilibria and Indirect Hard Modeling (IHM). Method A uses reported pKa values; method B estimates pKa = 3.59 via MCR-ALS and IHM fitting (close to literature). Calibration validated with leave-10%-out cross-validation; RMSECV ≤ 2.61 × 10⁻⁴ mol mol⁻¹.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No personal or sensitive information is included. All spectra originate from chemical samples without individual human identifiers.
rai:dataBiases: Spectra collected under ideal dilute mixture assumption (activity coefficients = 1) at a single instrument (Kaiser RXN2, 785 nm) and temperature (25 °C); models may not transfer without recalibration. Monoprotic system covers only two species (FA, FA⁻). Acid purity 98–100% (Merck); trace impurities may contribute minor spectral artefacts.
rai:dataLimitations: Models calibrated on NaOH titration in deionized water at 25 °C; not directly applicable to complex matrices, elevated temperatures, or concentrations outside calibration range. Ideal mixture assumption not strictly valid above 0.1 mol L⁻¹. Extrapolation to other instruments requires recalibration.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Formic acid is a C1 platform chemical with applications in hydrogen storage, biomass conversion, and preservation. Inline monitoring of its dissociation state supports real-time optimisation of synthesis and separation processes in the biobased chemical industry and electrochemical CO₂ reduction.
rai:dataUseCases: Inline monitoring of monoprotic acid dissociation in aqueous solution; validation case for the method B (unknown pKa) approach; IHM vs. PLS benchmarking; PAT in formic acid production and biobased acid separation processes.
